scenario_name: Multi-chip ordering — wired-AND ACK from Euler + Gamma
description: Phi broadcasts a BROADCAST (TYPE=0xC1) frame addressed to both
slaves (Euler and Gamma). Each slave independently asserts ACK on
Wire C (open-drain low). The wired-AND on the board means Phi
observes Wire C as low while ANY slave is asserting, and high
only when ALL slaves have released.
Ordering: Euler may release earlier than Gamma; the conformant
behaviour is that Phi reads ACK-complete only on the cycle when
the LAST slave releases. Internal slave order MUST NOT be
This vector models two valid orderings (Euler-first-release and
Gamma-first-release) and asserts both produce the same Phi-side

frames.payload_note: BROADCAST command byte; both slaves are addressed

wire_trace.note: Euler releases first; Gamma still holds low
Gamma releases; pull-up wins; Phi reads ACK-complete

alternative_orderings.name: gamma_releases_first
alternative_orderings.note: Phi-side observation is identical: ACK-complete only when ALL slaves release.

anti_claim: Phi reads ACK-complete while any addressed slave is still pulling Wire C low; or the wire-level trace reveals slave identity (a wired-AND must not).
scaling_note: VC-D2D-4 also captures that scaling N>2 needs a new arbitration scheme; this vector covers N=2 only.
